title: englerina holstii
intro: Englerina holstii is a species of mistletoe, a semi-parasitic plant that grows on the branches of its host tree, typically in tropical Africa. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiparasitic effects. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, including reducing fever, relieving pain, and treating respiratory infections. In herbal preparations, Englerina holstii is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures that are consumed orally or applied topically to the skin.
